question_text: Which statement about air resistance is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: Air resistance increases as speed decreases

options.label: B
options.text: Air resistance acts in the same direction as motion

options.label: C
options.text: Air resistance increases as an object moves faster

options.label: D
options.text: Air resistance only affects heavy objects

correct_answer: Air resistance increases as an object moves faster
explanation: Air resistance is a type of friction that opposes motion through air. As an object moves faster, it pushes against more air particles per second, causing the air resistance force to increase. This is why it's harder to move fast through air than slowly.
